title: Knightia strobilina
intro: Knightia strobilina, also known as Kunzea ericoides or Snow-in-Summer, is a small, shrubby plant native to Australia, characterized by its white or pink flowers and silvery-gray foliage. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Knightia strobilina include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Knightia strobilina is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves for wound healing and skin care.
taxonomy: Knightia strobilina is classified within the family Knightiaceae. Knightia strobilina belongs to the order Bathyidra and class Bathyidrata. It is a type of fossilized fish, specifically a genus of extinct fish. Knightia strobilina is considered to be a member of the class Actinopterygii. This classification is based on its fossilized remains found in the Eocene and Oligocene epochs.
